diagrams.title: Divide and Conquer Paradigm
diagrams.description: A visual representation of the general Divide and Conquer approach showing how a problem is divided into subproblems, conquered recursively, and combined to form the final solution.
elements: Arrows pointing down to smaller subproblems
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental three-step D&C paradigm (Divide, Conquer, Combine) that underlies both Merge Sort and Quick Sort algorithms

diagrams.title: Merge Sort Execution Flow
diagrams.description: A step-by-step visualization of Merge Sort showing the recursive splitting of an array into individual elements and the merging process that combines sorted subarrays back together.
elements: Binary tree structure showing recursive splitting
Merge operations showing comparison and ordering
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how Merge Sort divides the array recursively and then merges sorted halves, illustrating the O(n log n) time complexity concept

diagrams.title: Quick Sort Partitioning Process
diagrams.description: A visualization of Quick Sort showing the partitioning step where elements are rearranged around a pivot value, with smaller elements to the left and larger to the right.
elements: Initial array with highlighted pivot element
Arrows showing element swaps during partitioning
Pivot correctly positioned in sorted position
Recursion arrows to left and right subarrays
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the partition-based approach of Quick Sort, showing how the pivot selection and element rearrangement lead to sorted order, explaining the average O(n log n) complexity
